prs.title: feat(langs): support embeddedLanguages alias for backwards compatibility (#1044)
prs.body:
<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

### Description

This PR adds support for the embeddedLanguages field in LanguageRegistration.

Historically the field was named embeddedLangs, while VSCode grammars use embeddedLanguages.
Shiki currently supports only embeddedLangs, which prevents grammars using the VSCode naming convention from working as expected.

### Linked Issues
Fixes #1044

### Additional context

No breaking changes introduced — if both fields are present, embeddedLanguages takes priority.
This brings Shiki in line with VSCode terminology while preserving support for older grammars.

prs.title: Fix incorrect Ctrl+H shortcut description
prs.body:
This PR fixes a documentation issue where Ctrl+H was described as opening
the Keyboard Shortcuts panel.

Ctrl+H is intended to open the Docs page.
The shortcut description has now been updated accordingly.

prs.title: docs: fix typos in index.md and bundles.md
prs.body:
<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

### Description

This PR fixes two minor typos in the Shiki documentation for better clarity and correctness:

1. docs/guide/index.md (line 40): Fixed typo — 22th → 22nd
2. docs/guide/bundles.md (line 88): Fixed typo — avaliable → available

These are small documentation improvements and do not affect any functionality or code behavior.

prs.title: UI enhancements.
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Review forms now appear in a modal overlay with a close button for easier dismissal.
  * "No reviews yet" message is shown when a service has no reviews.

* **Style**
  * Major visual improvements to review forms, service cards, and service forms for a cleaner, more modern look.
  * Enhanced modal overlays, button styles, and responsive layouts.
  * Improved display of service meta information and clearer section headers.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Corrected display logic for service ratings and review messages.

* **Documentation**
  * Improved inline comments and section labeling for better clarity in service categories.

* **Chores**
  * Minor formatting and import path corrections for consistency.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Feature/UI enhancements
prs.body:
This PR includes multiple UI improvements to enhance the overall user experience and make the interface more modern and professional.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Review forms now appear in a polished modal dialog with a close button for improved user interaction.
  * Service cards now display "No reviews yet" when appropriate, and price/duration labels use the Indian Rupee symbol and clearer formatting.
  * Service forms include new section headers with emojis and enhanced placeholder texts for better clarity.

* **Style**
  * Major visual refinements to review forms, service cards, and service forms, including improved spacing, typography, modal overlays, and responsive design.
  * Enhanced dark mode support and subtle animations for a more modern look.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Corrected import paths and improved empty state messaging in the service category view.

* **Chores**
  * Removed multiple pages, components, stylesheets, and configuration files related to client-side UI and routing, including appointment management, user profile, authentication, and service category pages.
  * Deleted client-side documentation files and setup scripts.
  * Removed utility functions related to service ratings.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Updated the Projects Page.
prs.body:
This PR updates and enhances the Projects page to improve layout, design consistency, and user experience across the site.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Introduced client-side navigation using React Router for seamless page transitions.
  * Added a visually rich, interactive projects section with a hero carousel and horizontally scrollable project rows.

* **Style**
  * Added new CSS styles for the projects section and footer, enhancing visual appeal and interactivity.
  * Removed extensive unused CSS from the main application stylesheet.

* **Refactor**
  * Rewrote the projects page to feature categorized, scrollable project displays with improved navigation and user experience.
  * Updated navigation links in the homepage to use client-side routing.

* **Chores**
  * Added `react-router-dom` as a project dependency.

* **Revert**
  * Removed the standalone ProjectCard component.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Added the Projects page
prs.body:
I have added the projects page and linked it with homepage as well. Kindly review my PR.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Introduced a visually rich project showcase with a hero carousel and multiple horizontally scrollable project rows, including interactive navigation and category sections.

* **Refactor**
  * Updated navigation to use client-side routing for seamless page transitions.
  * Replaced manual page rendering with route-based rendering for improved structure and scalability.
  * Overhauled the projects page for a more engaging and interactive user experience.

* **Style**
  * Added a new, comprehensive CSS stylesheet for the projects section, featuring modern animations, responsive layouts, and enhanced visual effects.
  * Removed the previous main application CSS file, resulting in a refreshed visual design.

* **Chores**
  * Added `react-router-dom` as a new dependency for improved routing capabilities.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Navigation links now use client-side routing, preventing full page reloads when navigating between sections.

* **Revert**
  * Removed the standalone project card component, consolidating project display logic within the new projects showcase.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Initial template setup.
prs.body:
#### Fix #7 

This PR adds the initial template for the Project Showcase website using React + Vite. It includes a basic project structure with routing logic for the Home, Projects, and About pages. Currently, plain CSS is used for styling, but it can easily be extended with Tailwind CSS or any other styling approach later on. It includes basic layout setup using React components (Navbar, Footer, Home, Projects, About).

prs.title: Mention lowercase doctype in style guide
prs.body:
<!-- 🙌 Thanks for contributing to MDN Web Docs. Adding details below will help us to merge your PR faster. -->

### Description
<!-- ✍️ Summarize your changes in one or two sentences -->
Updated the <!doctype html> declaration to <!DOCTYPE html> in the HTML Code Style Guide to align with standard conventions and best practices.

### Motivation
<!-- ❓ Why are you making these changes and how do they help readers? -->
The uppercase <!DOCTYPE html> is the widely accepted format for declaring the document type in HTML5. Ensuring consistency helps avoid confusion among readers and follows best coding practices.

<!-- 👷‍♀️ After submitting, go to the "Checks" tab of your PR for the build status -->

prs.title: Updated glossary: clarified Future, AppFuture, and DataFuture; added …
prs.body:
# Description

This PR updates the glossary (`glossary.rst`) by clarifying the definitions of **Future, AppFuture, and DataFuture**.  
A **comparison table** has been added to improve clarity and provide a better understanding of their differences.  
These changes aim to enhance the documentation for new users who may find these terms confusing.

# Changed Behaviour

- Improved descriptions for **Future, AppFuture, and DataFuture** to be clearer and more concise.  
- Added a comparison table for easy differentiation.  
- No changes to core functionality, only an improvement in documentation.  

# Fixes

Fixes # (issue number, if applicable)  

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix  
- [ ] New feature  
- [x] Update to human readable text: Documentation/error messages/comments  
- [ ] Code maintenance/cleanup

prs.title: Added my name to the contributors list
prs.body:
Before submitting this pull request, check the changes to see it's only the changes you made intentionallly
If there are changes to other lines you didn't make deliberately, it's possible that your IDE made the changes with a utility like prettier.
Next time, make sure that you only add your changes by using `git add -p` and rather than `git add Contributors.md`

If you're doing something in the checklist below, put an `x` inside `[ ]` so that `- [ ]` becomes `- [x]`

- [x] I had fun going through this tutorial (ノ^o^)ノ and learned on the way ٩(＾◡＾)۶
- [x] There are some things I'd like to improve in this tutorial. I have written them below.
- [x] There were steps where I had errors while following this tutorial. I have written them below.

issues.title: [BUG]Ctrl+H shortcut opens Getting Started instead of Shortcuts page
issues.body: **Describe the bug** Ctrl+H shortcut is to Open shortcuts but it opens Getting Started instead of Shortcuts page **Steps to Reproduce** 1. Open the drawDB editor. 2. Press `Ctrl + H`. 3. Observe that it navigates to "Getting Started" instead of "Keyboard Shortcuts". **Expected behavior** Pressing `Ctrl + H` should open the **Keyboard Shortcuts** page. **Screenshots** As you can see ctrl+H is for opening the shortcuts This is the Screen Recording for the required bug. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/affef86c-66ab-4d6b-a186-02a8c5aa9b16 Desktop: OS: macOS Browser: Google Chrome (latest) Reproducible: Yes (both normal and incognito mode)

issues.title: Feature: Contact Form UI/UX Improvements (Placeholders & Animations)
issues.body: Improve the user experience and modern aesthetic of the Contact Us page by adding standard accessibility features (placeholders) and modern visual feedback Proposed Changes: 1. Placeholder Implementation (UX/Accessibility) Adding placeholder text provides immediate context for the expected input in each field, improving initial user clarity. 2. UI/Animation Enhancements Adding subtle, modern animations will make the form more engaging and provide clear feedback on user interaction @nagcas I would like to work on this issue under Hacktoberfest 2025 If i get assigned. Here is the current Contact Us page and you can see it lacks placeholders.

issues.title: Enhancement: Modernize UI of Personal Finance Tracker Page.
issues.body:
📝 Description
I’ll redesign it with a modern, clean, and responsive layout inspired by glassy aesthetic.

🎨 Proposed Changes
- Apply glassmorphism effects to cards and sections
- Use subtle gradients, rounded corners, and modern typography
- Improve button and input styling with hover transitions
- Make layout responsive using Flexbox/Grid
- Retain and refine existing animations for a smoother experience

🧠 Tech Stack
- HTML5
- CSS3

✅ Expected Result
A professional, minimal, and visually appealing dashboard-style UI that enhances user experience.

@neeraj542 Can you assign me this under Hacktoberfest 2025 I would like to work on this issue.

issues.title: Remove redundant “Contact” button for cleaner UI
issues.body: Currently, the navigation bar already includes a Contact link. The additional Contact button on the top-right corner is redundant and may confuse users. Suggestion: Remove the extra Contact button in the top-right corner. Keep the navigation Contact link for consistency. Can you assign me this issue under Hacktoberfest?
